briefing.opening: Good morning/afternoon. I will now begin the climate briefing for June 18, 2026.

sections.title: 🔴 Deepening Climate Crisis and the Need for Urgent Response
sections.content: Key news announced today emphasizes the deepening global climate crisis and the urgent need for a response. The World Meteorological Organization (WMO) has projected that Asia's average temperature in 2025 will rank between the second and fourth highest on record, warning of intensifying heatwaves, floods, and ocean warming [6]. Predictions also indicate that if global temperatures rise by 4℃, river flows will become unstable, leading to alternating floods and droughts [14]. Notably, climate change is causing more local extinctions in temperate regions than in tropical regions [126], and a human rights organization's warning that it threatens livelihoods in Honduras is also noteworthy [128]. According to a UN report, climate change and pollution are pushing oceans to a tipping point [136], and real-world damage cases have already been reported, such as more than half of Aiyetoro, Nigeria, being eroded and lost to the sea [137].

sections.title: 🌱 Expansion of Domestic Carbon Neutrality Practices and Adaptation Efforts
sections.content: Various local governments across the country are actively pursuing efforts to respond to climate change and implement carbon neutrality. Jeju Province has launched a research project to develop job creation measures in response to AI and climate change [1, 25, 58], while Anseong City is conducting research to select waxy corn varieties that adapt to climate change [4]. Cheongju Carbon Neutrality Support Center operates a Carbon Neutrality Academy [7], and Chungju City is accelerating citizen-led carbon neutrality projects and its transition to a 'Carbon Neutral Green City' [30, 31, 32, 38, 46, 51, 55, 61, 63, 71, 72, 74]. Notably, Cheongsong County achieved a reduction of 140 tons of greenhouse gases by providing carbon neutrality incentives to 276 households that practiced energy saving [29, 39, 40, 42, 48, 52, 57, 68, 69]. The National Institute of Forest Science is also working to establish a scientific foundation by presenting directions for future forests in the era of climate change [12] and initiating monitoring of climate change-causing substances in the inland regions of the Korean Peninsula [5, 20, 27]. Companies are also taking on the challenge towards carbon neutrality, and international efforts continue, with ISO, for example, publishing a draft of carbon neutrality standards [34, 53].

sections.title: ♻️ Accelerating Renewable Energy Transition and Facing Challenges
sections.content: To accelerate the domestic energy transition, five thermal power generation companies are pursuing re-integration after 25 years, speeding up the expansion of renewable energy [76, 103, 109]. The Korea Electrical Contractors Association has also launched a specialized committee for new and renewable energy to improve systems and expand rights [78, 82]. Korea Southern Power (KOSPO) has launched a 'Renewable Energy Business Development Strategic Advisory Council' and established an 11.2 GW roadmap by 2040 [84, 90, 104]. Financial institutions are also actively investing, with Shinhan Bank and Korea Development Bank completing 241 billion KRW in project financing for the creation of a 300 MW solar power cluster in Yeonggwang, Jeollanam-do [79, 110, 112, 117, 118]. However, the expansion of renewable energy also presents challenges. Concerns have been raised about the infringement of rural residential rights due to standardized setback distances for solar power facilities [83]. Furthermore, a gap between planning and execution is evident, as Australia, despite securing 32 GW of renewable energy generation capacity, has only commenced construction on approximately 7 GW [85]. Additionally, Incheon's electricity self-sufficiency rate decreased by 41%p over 10 years, emphasizing the need for a phased expansion of new and renewable energy [87]. It has also been pointed out that the current system of five power generation companies has limitations in phasing out coal and transitioning to renewable energy [101, 119]. Despite these challenges, innovative attempts continue, such as Hyundai Motor Group's initiative to transition Antarctic science stations to eco-friendly energy with a green hydrogen grid [106, 113]. Furthermore, a report in April indicated that global wind and solar power generation surpassed gas power generation [115].

sections.title: 🌍 Enhancing Climate Change Awareness and Scientific Understanding
sections.content: Public awareness of climate change is deepening globally. According to a Yale University study, despite former President Trump's claims denying climate change, the American public feels the necessity to address it [3]. International cooperation and research are also active, with Lee Chang-hoon, Co-Chair of the Climate Response Committee, attending London Climate Action Week [8], and Emperor Naruhito inspecting a Dutch water research institute, expressing interest in climate change response cooperation [10], demonstrating continued international solidarity. Scientists are meeting to seek solutions to high-altitude climate change threats [127], and hopeful research findings are emerging, such as the discovery of coral reefs capable of surviving climate change [130]. Businesses are also beginning to recognize the difficult reality of climate change and natural loss [134]. In the field of literature, the discourse on climate change is expanding across society, with claims that beyond the 'cli-fi' genre, all stories can be interpreted as climate change narratives [125].

briefing.closing: Today's briefing has highlighted the severity of the climate crisis, along with various domestic and international efforts and new challenges to overcome it. This is a time when our efforts for a sustainable future are becoming even more crucial. This concludes today's climate briefing.
